---
title: Namespace
description: Create isolated sub-caches with a shared adapter using key prefixes.
icon: FolderOpen
---

## What is a namespace?

A namespace is a scoped sub-cache built on top of the same adapter. Every key operation is automatically prefixed with `prefix:`, so different namespaces cannot collide.

```ts
const userCache = cache.namespace("user");
const sessionCache = cache.namespace("session");

await userCache.set("1", userData);       // stored as "user:1"
await sessionCache.set("1", sessionData); // stored as "session:1"
```

## Creating a namespace

```ts
const ns = cache.namespace("prefix");

await ns.set("key", value, { ttl: "5m" });
const value = await ns.get("key");
await ns.delete("key");
await ns.has("key");
```

All `CacheInstance` methods are available on the namespace object.

## Namespace options

`cache.namespace(prefix, opts?)` accepts an optional second argument to override defaults for the sub-cache:

```ts
const ns = cache.namespace("session", {
  ttl: "30m",       // default TTL for this namespace
  onError: "throw", // override error handling
});
```

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `ttl` | `TTL` | Default TTL for `set` / `wrap` in this namespace |
| `tags` | `string[]` | Default tags for entries in this namespace |
| `onError` | `OnError` | Error handling for this namespace |

The separator between prefix and key is always `:` — it is not configurable.

## Nested namespaces

Namespaces can be nested — each adds a prefix layer:

```ts
const org = cache.namespace("org:42");
const orgPosts = org.namespace("posts");

await orgPosts.set("1", post); // stored as "org:42:posts:1"
```

## Namespace plugin

For static namespaces applied at cache creation time, use the `namespacePlugin` instead of calling `cache.namespace()`. See [Plugins](/docs/cache/plugins) for details.
